channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Governor Radda Launches Cassava Processing Hub to Boost Katsina State Economy
item.description: Governor AbdulRahman Bello Radda of Katsina State has unveiled a state-of-the-art cassava/gari processing centre in Batsari Local Government Area, aiming to transform the agricultural sector and creat...

item.title: Sharon Otieno's Sister Confronts Grief as Court Delivers Final Verdict in Eight-Year Murder Case
item.description: Sharon Otieno's sister, @whi_tney14, posted an emotional TikTok tribute ahead of the final ruling in her sister's 2018 murder case, which has dragged on for eight years. The case, linking former Migor...

item.title: Canada's Track and Field Stars Shine at Commonwealth Games
item.description: The article previews Canada's top track and field athletes competing in the 2026 Commonwealth Games in Glasgow. Key highlights include the men's 4x100m relay team, led by Andre De Grasse, aiming for t...

item.title: strengthening pak-iran relations: president pezeshkian's call for regional unity
item.description: iranian president masoud pezeshkian emphasized the importance of strengthening pak-iran relations during a ceremony where he received the credentials of pakistan's new ambassador to iran, imran ahmed ...

item.title: Dispute Over Bullet Trains and Traditional Railways in India
item.description: The article discusses the ongoing debate in India regarding the introduction of bullet trains as a modern transportation solution versus the traditional railway system. The Indian government has been ...

item.title: Trump's White House Bunker and Paranoia: Expert Analysis on Strongman Mentality
item.description: The article discusses historian Ruth Ben-Ghiat's analysis of former President Donald Trump's obsession with constructing a bunker beneath his White House ballroom, linking it to the behavior of author...

item.title: Hiker's Journey on the Pacific Crest Trail: Challenges and Encounters
item.description: This article chronicles a hiker's experiences on the Pacific Crest Trail during days 16-20 of their journey. The narrative highlights the challenges of navigating sketchy creek crossings, dealing with...

item.title: Shop Owner Clarifies Misunderstanding Over Identity Documents Found in Store
item.description: A South African shop owner near the Department of Home Affairs on Umgeni Road has addressed a viral social media incident where a group accused him of possessing eight identity documents. The confront...

item.title: Legal battle over Philippine sovereignty in South China Sea
item.description: Former Supreme Court Justice Antonio Carpio asserted that China cannot legally prevent the Philippines from constructing facilities at Ayungin Shoal, which is part of the country's exclusive economic ...

item.title: Arokodare's Protest Leads to Wolves Training Cancellation
item.description: Super Eagles striker Tolu Arokodare sparked controversy by sitting on the pitch during Wolves' pre-season training, demanding a transfer to Fiorentina after his bid was rejected. The 25-year-old joine...

item.title: Elon Musk's AI Project Aims to Create Historically Accurate 'The Odyssey' by End of 2026
item.description: Elon Musk has announced that his Grok AI tool will produce a 'historically accurate' full-length adaptation of Homer's 'The Odyssey' by the end of 2026. The claim follows his public criticism of Chris...

item.title: Adebayo Jolaoso: Bridging Art and Documentation in Photography
item.description: This article explores Adebayo Jolaoso's photography career, highlighting his unique ability to blend fine art with documentary styles. Born and based in Lagos, Jolaoso's work spans diverse genres incl...

item.title: Government addresses Rahul Gandhi's paper leak allegations, questions selective outrage over exam irregularities
item.description: The Indian government, through Union Minister JP Nadda, addressed allegations by Congress leader Rahul Gandhi regarding 152 paper leaks in the NEET-UG exam over the past decade. Nadda stated that the ...

item.title: Nissan SA Focuses on Import Strategy to Regain Market Share in South Africa
item.description: Nissan South Africa has completed its transition from a local manufacturer to a national sales company, shifting focus to rebuilding market share through imported models. Managing director Juan Wheele...

item.title: Albanese to begin consultation on a new Fair Work court for small workplace claims
item.description: Prime Minister Anthony Albanese is expected to announce plans to begin consultation on establishing a new Fair Work court designed to provide workers with a simpler and more accessible way to resolve ...

item.title: Halifax and 8 Other Canadian Cities to Screen Christopher Nolan's 'The Odyssey' in 70mm Imax
item.description: Christopher Nolan's epic film 'The Odyssey' is being shown in its original 70mm Imax format in only nine Canadian cities, with Halifax's Scotiabank Theatre being one of them. The film, the first ever ...

item.title: Mexican Mayor Killed During Town Hall Meeting
item.description: A Mexican mayor was fatally shot during a town hall meeting, according to reports by Agence France-Presse. The incident occurred in a public setting, raising concerns about security and political stab...

item.title: Gachagua Outlines Vote-Based Strategy for Opposition Leader Selection Ahead of 2027 Election
item.description: The article details intense political activity in Kenya ahead of the 2027 General Election, focusing on emerging opposition alliances and efforts to determine President William Ruto's strongest rival....

item.title: US and Saudi Arabia Sign Civilian Nuclear Cooperation Agreement with Non-Proliferation Safeguards
item.description: The United States and Saudi Arabia have signed a landmark agreement to establish a civilian nuclear program in Saudi Arabia, with Washington emphasizing that the deal includes non-proliferation safegu...

item.title: Maduro and wife face June 2027 trial in US over drug charges
item.description: Venezuela's former president, Nicolas Maduro, and his wife Cilia Flores are set to face trial in the US in June 2027 after being captured in a military raid in January 2026. The couple has been held i...
